questions.question: How often should the fire risk assessment normally be reviewed to keep it up to date?
options: At least once a year
Once every five years
Every three months
Once a month

questions.question: Class B fires involve which of the following?
options: Cooking oils and deep fat
Flammable liquids such as petrol or spirits
Solids such as wood and paper
Live electrical equipment

questions.question: Why is a dry powder extinguisher a poor choice for use indoors?
options: It leaves a thick sticky residue on skin
It creates a choking cloud that is unpleasant and hard to see through
It only works on electrical fires
It makes flammable liquids spread faster

questions.question: Which extinguisher is suitable for a Class A fire involving paper, wood or fabric?
options: Wet chemical only
Such a fire cannot be extinguished
Water or foam
Carbon dioxide only

questions.question: When using an extinguisher, where should you aim the nozzle?
options: At the base of the fire
At the smoke above the fire
At the ceiling directly overhead
At the top of the flames

questions.question: In a fire alarm system, manual call points are the...?
options: Red 'break-glass' boxes at exits and along corridors
Fire alarm control panel
Green emergency exit signs
Automatic smoke detectors

questions.question: A fire door only does its job of holding back fire and smoke when it is...?
options: Closed
Painted a bright colour
Removed from its hinges
Wedged open for easy access

questions.question: If a fire door genuinely needs to be held open for daily use, what must be fitted?
options: A simple metal hook
An approved automatic release device that closes it when the alarm sounds
A rubber wedge
A rope tie-back

questions.question: After a fire, who decides when it is safe to re-enter the building?
options: Any senior member of staff on duty
The registered manager
The fire marshal
Only the fire service

questions.question: According to the material, what is the single biggest cause of fire in care homes?
options: Cooking
Candles
Arson
Electrical faults and appliances

questions.question: The fire warden (fire marshal) role is best described as...?
options: A support and co-ordination role, not a firefighting one
A medical first-aid role
A role that is only active during planned drills
A firefighting role, tackling the blaze directly

questions.question: What is a Personal Emergency Evacuation Plan (PEEP)?
options: A written, individual plan recording exactly how a specific person will be helped to safety
A general fire procedure covering the whole building
A map of fire extinguisher locations
The schedule for the weekly alarm test

questions.question: When you call 999 for the fire service from a care home, what should you do?
options: Assume a colleague has already called
Wait until the fire is out before calling
Give the full address, say it is a care home, and say if anyone is trapped or has restricted mobility
Give only the postcode to save time

questions.question: Where should battery equipment such as phones and mobility scooters be charged?
options: Inside a closed wardrobe
On a hard surface, and unplugged once charged
On a resident's bed for convenience
On a soft sofa

questions.question: A fire door will still hold back fire and smoke effectively even when it is propped open.
options: True
False

questions.question: Wet chemical extinguishers are designed for Class F fires, such as cooking oils and deep fat.
options: True
False

questions.question: Normal washing fully removes emollient residue from clothing and bedding.
options: True
False

questions.question: Carbon dioxide (CO2) extinguishers suit live electrical equipment and leave no residue.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of these are your own duties in relation to the fire risk assessment and fire procedures? (Select all that apply)
options: Report faults, missing signs or blocked exits straight away
Take part in the training and fire drills your employer arranges
Decide the building's overall evacuation strategy
Work in line with the fire procedures the assessment leads to
Personally sign off the annual fire risk assessment

questions.question: Which duties belong to a fire marshal or warden during an evacuation? (Select all that apply)
options: Coordinate the evacuation and direct people to the exits
Check rooms, toilets and communal areas are clear where it is safe to do so
Enter the fire compartment to put the fire out themselves
Carry out a headcount or roll call at the assembly point
Decide when the building can be re-entered

questions.question: Which of these are recognised ways to reduce the fire risk from emollient creams? (Select all that apply)
options: Choose only paraffin-free emollients, which carry no fire risk
Record the risk in the person's care plan
Wash contact clothing, bedding and towels regularly, daily where possible
Apply extra emollient at bedtime to protect the skin overnight
Keep the person well away from cigarettes, lighters and naked flames

questions.question: Once everyone is out and the fire service has taken over, what should still be done? (Select all that apply)
options: Keep those you care for warm, calm and reassured
Re-enter as soon as the visible flames are out to check the rooms
Account for people and tell the fire service immediately about anyone unaccounted for
Go back inside to collect residents' belongings
Report what happened so the incident is recorded and the fire risk assessment reviewed

questions.question: Wet chemical extinguishers are made for Class ___ fires, such as cooking oils and deep fat.
In the UK, extinguisher bodies are ___ with a coloured label or band showing the type.
Moving residents sideways through fire doors into an adjoining protected compartment on the same floor is called progressive ___ evacuation.
